overview: Since the witest28-8 system consists of exactly one self-contained component with no inter-service dependencies, the entire implementation is delivered in a single phase. There is no dependency graph to resolve, so all work can proceed immediately without waiting on any upstream services or shared infrastructure. This single-phase approach minimizes overhead and reflects the simplicity of the architecture.

phases.name: Foundation Service
phases.description: The sole component of the system — a self-contained Go HTTP service with no external dependencies. Implements all routes, security hardening, and packaging in one deliverable.

components.name: hello-world-service
responsibilities: Serve a fully compliant Hello World HTML5 page at the root path (GET /) with WCAG 2.1 Level A accessibility and responsive layout.
Expose a health check endpoint (GET /health) returning a JSON status payload for container orchestration liveness probes.
Return a properly structured 404 HTML response for all undefined routes.
Strip server-identifying HTTP response headers (e.g. Server) to prevent internal version/path disclosure.
Package the application as a minimal distroless Docker image via a multi-stage build for a fast, secure container startup.
components.architectureContext: This is the only component in the system and has no upstream or downstream service dependencies. It is directly accessed by end users via a browser (or ingress proxy in non-local environments) and by platform health-check mechanisms via /health. All logic — routing, HTML rendering, security middleware — is self-contained within this single Go binary.
keyConsiderations: The net/http ServeMux treats '/' as a catch-all prefix; an explicit path equality check (r.URL.Path != "/") is required to prevent sub-paths from incorrectly matching the root handler and must fall through to a 404.
Only GET is a valid method for both '/' and '/health'; POST, PUT, DELETE, and other methods must be rejected with HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed to avoid silent misuse.
A response-writer middleware must overwrite or delete the 'Server' header on every outgoing response before WriteHeader is called, since Go's net/http sets this automatically.
The multi-stage Dockerfile must produce a CGO_ENABLED=0 static binary compatible with the distroless/static:nonroot base image — no shell, no libc, no extra attack surface.

The inline HTML must include a viewport meta tag and sufficient color contrast (body #111111 on #ffffff exceeds the 4.5:1 WCAG 2.1 Level AA ratio) to satisfy both responsiveness and accessibility requirements without any external assets that could slow page load.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3 info: title: Hello World Service API description: Serves the Hello World web page and health endpoint. version: 1.0.0 paths: /: get: summary: Hello World page description: Returns the main Hello World HTML page. responses: '200': description: HTML page with Hello World greeting content: text/html: schema: type: string example: 'Hello World

Hello World

' /health: get: summary: Health check description: Returns 200 OK if the service is running. responses: '200': description: Service is healthy content: application/json: schema: type: object properties: status: type: string example: ok /{path}: get: summary: Catch-all 404 description: Returns 404 for any undefined route. parameters: - name: path in: path required: true schema: type: string responses: '404': description: Route not found content: text/html: schema: type: string
